extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity) requires that setting dyspnea=true, with all other inputs held fixed, never lowers acuity. It is violated because the interaction_drop rule (priority 100, the highest) fires whenever dyspnea is true and forces acuity := self_care (the lowest level), overriding every escalating rule.

Counterexample: hold fever=true, dizziness=false.
- Baseline dyspnea=false: no rule sets acuity except raise_fever (priority 3) -> acuity = emergent (the highest level). interaction_drop does not match since dyspnea is false.
- Flip dyspnea=true (the witness assignment): interaction_drop (priority 100) and raise_fever (priority 3) both match. Highest priority wins -> acuity = self_care (the lowest level).

acuity goes from emergent down to self_care when dyspnea is set true, with fever and dizziness unchanged. Since self_care < emergent in the listed enum order, setting dyspnea true lowered acuity, violating the property. The witness_assignment gives the full input vector at the violating (dyspnea=true) state.
